mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward test shows that months with positive RSAFS growth do not produce statistically significant positive 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned at or below 0.5 percent, markets price an extended period of near-zero discount rates and liquidity support, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative more than 50% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment and falling industrial production jointly signal a contractionary gap where labor market deterioration coincides with output decline, increasing recession probability and pushing S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises month-over-month and INDPRO falls month-over-month
failure_condition: When both UNRATE increases and INDPRO decreases, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observed instances, or the interaction term adds no explanatory power (incremental R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: In an elevated inflation regime, month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index causes markets to reprice expected monetary tightening and erodes real earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: During months when CPIAUCSL exceeds 280, positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of instances, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production serves as a real-time proxy for aggregate supply and capacity utilization; sustained expansion reduces unit costs and supports profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive changes in INDPRO do not correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (Spearman correlation < 0.03) over a 3-year rolling window, or returns are not higher than median baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.8 million units signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials, furnishings, and credit, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1800, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered months, or the mean forward return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of unemployment below 4 percent and a rising federal funds rate indicates that monetary policy is tightening into an already tight labor market, increasing the risk of a hard landing and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE is below 4% and FEDFUNDS is rising do not produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve classification accuracy (delta AUC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly growth in real GDP reflects broad-based demand contraction that corporate earnings cannot offset through margin management, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter when the economy is in an early recession.
failure_condition: Quarters with negative GDPC1 growth show positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean forward return is not significantly below the full-sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).
